jokes.text: Good show, Liz Lemon! You coming to the after-after party. We have after-after parties?
jokes.setup: Character invites Liz to an after-after party
jokes.punchline: Liz's surprise that such a thing exists
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation joke that establishes the show business party culture absurdity

jokes.text: You going to the after-after-after-after party?
jokes.setup: Previous after-after party joke
jokes.punchline: Escalating to an absurd number of 'afters'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but predictable structure

jokes.text: It's about to turn back into a taxi dispatch center
jokes.setup: Suggesting they leave the venue
jokes.punchline: Absurd revelation about what the venue normally is
jokes.explanation: Classic 30 Rock absurdist detail that's both random and specific

jokes.text: Don't go, Liz Lemon! There's still a after-after- after-after-after-party! I just gotta take my kids to soccer first.
jokes.setup: Continuing the after-party escalation
jokes.punchline: Mundane family obligation interrupting the party chain
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's scattered priorities and lifestyle

jokes.text: Must've been Angie Harmon
jokes.setup: Jack realizes he didn't tell Liz about his vineyard
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions telling Angie Harmon instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack character moment - casually name-dropping celebrities as if they're close friends

jokes.text: When I was eight, I had my name on the score board at a Phillies game. And they spelled it 'Lez' but it was pretty cool.
jokes.setup: Liz relating to Jack's excitement about having his name on something
jokes.punchline: Her pathetic childhood achievement with unfortunate misspelling
jokes.explanation: Excellent Liz moment showing her sad past and tendency to overshare embarrassing details

jokes.text: This ought to prove my mother wrong, saying that Donaghy is Gaelic for 'failure.'
jokes.setup: Jack's excitement about his wine venture
jokes.punchline: Revealing his mother's cruel definition of their family name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack backstory joke revealing his mother issues and family dysfunction

jokes.text: She's a Murphy. Bunch of mud farmers and sheep rapists.
jokes.setup: Jack dismissing his mother's opinion
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurdly specific ethnic slurs about her family
jokes.explanation: Peak Alec Baldwin delivery - escalating from casual dismissal to shocking specificity

jokes.text: Oh, I'm gonna vomit! What the hell am I gonna do? I've got 10,000 cases of this crap.
jokes.setup: Jack tasting his own wine
jokes.punchline: Immediate horrified reaction and realization of scale
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment setting up the wine plot

jokes.text: Dewey, Cheatham, and Livingston
jokes.setup: Jack mentioning Steven's investment firm
jokes.punchline: Law firm name that sounds like 'Do We Cheat 'Em and Live in Sin'
jokes.explanation: Classic old joke but fits the corporate satire well

jokes.text: A black? That is offensive. No, no-- That's his last name. Steven Black.
jokes.setup: Jack saying Steven is 'a Black'
jokes.punchline: Liz's outrage followed by Jack's clarification it's his surname
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke that sets up the episode's racial comedy theme

jokes.text: Remarkable people, the Blacks. Musical, very athletic. Not very good swimmers. Again, I'm talking about the family.
jokes.setup: Jack praising the Black family
jokes.punchline: Accidentally listing racial stereotypes then clarifying he means the family
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the name confusion with perfect Jack obliviousness to his own racial implications

jokes.text: When I was dating Condoleezza, there were genuine cultural tensions. I mean, we would go to the movies, and she would yell at the screen.
jokes.setup: Jack claiming to have dated Condoleezza Rice
jokes.punchline: Reducing 'cultural tensions' to her talking during movies
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment combining name-dropping with complete tone-deafness about race relations

jokes.text: You know, when I leave work at night, I am just riding on a subway car full of scary, teenaged people.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming not to notice racial differences
jokes.punchline: Immediately revealing her own casual prejudices about subway riders
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Liz's hypocrisy about being colorblind while having other prejudices

jokes.text: Ridikolus-- the hip-hop producer? Isn't that the guy that bit Suge Knight? Yeah, yeah-- He bit Suge Knight. Held Raven Simone over a balcony. Made Rasheed Wallace cry.
jokes.setup: Introducing the intimidating hip-hop producer Ridikolus
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of increasingly absurd violent acts
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from plausible to absurd, with great specific details like making Rasheed Wallace cry

jokes.text: Don't let no one in who's not on the list 'Cause this mess is gonna get raw like sushi, so haters to the left.
jokes.setup: Tracy giving party security instructions
jokes.punchline: Mixing tough-guy talk with food metaphors and Destiny's Child references
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tracy's confused attempts at hip-hop toughness

jokes.text: What's your game, man? Boggle.
jokes.setup: Threatening question about someone's intentions
jokes.punchline: Kenneth innocently mentioning his favorite board game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - complete innocence in the face of street intimidation

jokes.text: With that guy that sent you the flowers? By mistake?
jokes.setup: Jenna asking about Liz's date
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that the flowers were sent by mistake
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds to Liz's romantic failures

jokes.text: The old leather pumpkin?
jokes.setup: Flower guy seeing Liz's basketball
jokes.punchline: Describing it as an old leather pumpkin
jokes.explanation: Great visual description that's both accurate and absurd

jokes.text: Why am I the only person that doesn't care that he's black?
jokes.setup: Jenna mentioning Steven is black
jokes.punchline: Liz's frustrated claim about being colorblind while obsessing over race
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing Liz's self-unawareness about her own racial fixation

jokes.text: You're lookin' at a ghost, JD. Dead Man Walking, The Green Mile, Christmas With the Klumps.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he's like a ghost/dead man
jokes.punchline: Listing movies ending with the non-sequitur 'Christmas With the Klumps'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment mixing dramatic movie titles with his own random filmography

jokes.text: Growing up, when you were mad at somebody, you would just break dance at 'em. I'm talking about break hard, pull it back-- pop, boom, boom-- Into gladiator-- Bring it back down.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining old school conflict resolution
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of aggressive breakdancing moves
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy character moment with physical comedy and his unique worldview

jokes.text: contains no lead and is not fatal if swallowed
jokes.setup: Jack reading lab report about his wine
jokes.punchline: The extremely low bar for wine quality standards
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurdly low quality standards

jokes.text: I don't want my kids to have to go to college.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking Jack to take care of his family if he gets rich
jokes.punchline: Expressing this as not wanting his kids to go to college
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy moment showing his backwards priorities and nouveau riche aspirations

jokes.text: Is that a piece of corn in there?
jokes.setup: Tracy and Jack toasting with the wine
jokes.punchline: Tracy noticing solid food particles in the wine
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out gag reinforcing how terrible the wine is

jokes.text: You better watch out. Don't make a flap. 'Cause Hilary's wearing a Yankees cap.
jokes.setup: Steven talking about Mark Russell's political comedy
jokes.punchline: Singing an incredibly dated and unfunny political song
jokes.explanation: Intentionally bad comedy showing Steven's terrible taste in humor

jokes.text: What do you sit and look at?
jokes.setup: Steven saying he doesn't own a TV
jokes.punchline: Liz's practical question about what he stares at instead
jokes.explanation: Good Liz line showing her practical, TV-obsessed mindset

jokes.text: Not the fantasy movie with the, uh, monsters. I'm talking about the Strategic Defense Initiative.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning she likes Star Wars
jokes.punchline: Steven clarifying he means Reagan's missile defense program, not the movie
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Steven's nerdy political interests versus pop culture

jokes.text: Donaghy Estates tastes like the urine of Satan after a hefty portion of asparagus.
jokes.setup: Jack quoting a wine review
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and disgusting description
jokes.explanation: Incredible escalation with perfect specificity - becomes quotable instantly

jokes.text: Well, pick out a stick. 'Cause Ridikolus is gonna be drinking Donaghy Estates tonight.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking if Jack has more money than he could shake a stick at
jokes.punchline: Jack literalizing the expression
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay moment showing Jack's literal-minded approach to idioms

jokes.text: Wow, that would've been a great way to shoot Tracy.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentioning elevator maintenance
jokes.punchline: Ridikolus casually noting it as an assassination opportunity
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy showing the casual violence in Ridikolus's worldview

jokes.text: He's very unstable. Eh, did you get that at a cane shop?
jokes.setup: Jack warning about Kenneth being unstable
jokes.punchline: Ridikolus thinks he means Kenneth's walking cane
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - mental instability vs. physical mobility aid

jokes.text: Did you get that at a cane shop?
jokes.setup: Ridikolus seeing Kenneth's appearance
jokes.punchline: Asking where he got his cane in a casual, non-threatening way
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast showing Ridikolus's surprisingly normal curiosity

jokes.text: Yeah, but when you see it on an airplane, they take out all the swears and stuff, and it makes it so much funnier, I think.
jokes.setup: Steven referencing Zoolander
jokes.punchline: His bizarre theory that censorship improves comedy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Steven's backwards understanding of humor

jokes.text: We should take a carriage ride around the financial district.
jokes.setup: Steven suggesting romantic activity
jokes.punchline: Bizarre location choice for carriage ride
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Steven's unromantic nature

jokes.text: I get it. You don't like me, because I am black!
jokes.setup: Liz trying to end their date politely
jokes.punchline: Steven immediately playing the race card
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the race misunderstanding plot

jokes.text: I love black men! I love you!
jokes.setup: Liz being accused of racism
jokes.punchline: Her panicked overcorrection declaring love for black men
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Liz's panic about appearing racist

jokes.text: No, no-- not that kind of chocolate.
jokes.setup: Steven ordering Death by Chocolate dessert
jokes.punchline: Liz clarifying it's not a racial reference
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing Liz now seeing race in everything

jokes.text: Maybe if I just hang out with him four or five more teams, he'll see on his own that we are a bad match.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining her dating strategy
jokes.punchline: Saying 'teams' instead of 'times'
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism that fits Liz's scattered mental state

jokes.text: Ah, than Ahman-da. It's Amanda.
jokes.setup: Kenneth trying to pronounce Amanda's name
jokes.punchline: His exaggerated, incorrect pronunciation and her correction
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth character moment showing his odd speech patterns

jokes.text: Tom Cruise, 'cause that's how Oprah says it. 'Tom!' That's good; sounds like her.
jokes.setup: Tracy practicing celebrity impressions
jokes.punchline: Referencing Oprah's famous Tom Cruise couch-jumping moment
jokes.explanation: Decent pop culture reference showing Tracy's celebrity obsession

jokes.text: Shooting people at the Source Awards is a tradition. It's like Christmas. Or shooting people outside of Hot 97.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining why he won't host the Source Awards
jokes.punchline: Comparing award show violence to Christmas and other shooting locations
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy escalating from Christmas to more shooting locations

jokes.text: I called his pitbull a gaywad on 106 & Park.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his beef with Young Jeezy
jokes.punchline: Specific ridiculous insult on a music show
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy moment with specific, absurd beef origin story

jokes.text: We were both cast members on a Nickelodeon show called Ray Ray's Mystery Garage.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his beef with Raw Dog
jokes.punchline: Their conflict originated on a children's TV show
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd backstory connecting gangsta rap beef to children's television

jokes.text: See, it's a Catch 22! Aw, he's gonna be there too.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his impossible situation
jokes.punchline: Thinking Catch-22 is also a rapper who'll be at the awards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy moment misunderstanding literary reference as another rapper threat

jokes.text: What color plane you want to buy? Clear... like Wonder Woman's.
jokes.setup: Ridikolus talking about buying planes
jokes.punchline: Wanting an invisible plane like Wonder Woman
jokes.explanation: Great absurd moment mixing gangsta posturing with comic book references

jokes.text: I'm just going through the classic stages of grief. Fear... Denial... Horniness... Wisdom, sleepiness-- And now, depression.
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his emotional state
jokes.punchline: Listing stages of grief with absurd additions like 'horniness'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation mixing real psychology with Tracy's bizarre worldview

jokes.text: Aren't you the man who told me to live every week like it's Shark Week? And that nothing's impossible except for dinosaurs.
jokes.setup: Kenneth quoting Tracy's previous advice
jokes.punchline: Absurd life philosophies Tracy apparently shared
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth-Tracy moment with quotable Tracy wisdom that's both inspiring and nonsensical

jokes.text: The manatee has become the mento.
jokes.setup: Kenneth inspiring Tracy with his own advice
jokes.punchline: Tracy's mangled version of 'student becomes the teacher'
jokes.explanation: Great Tracy malapropism that still makes poetic sense in context

jokes.text: I found this really neat Canadian restaurant in Times Square.
jokes.setup: Steven suggesting restaurant
jokes.punchline: Oxymoronic Canadian restaurant in tourist trap location
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Steven's complete lack of taste

jokes.text: I truly don't like you as a person.
jokes.setup: Liz trying to convince Steven it's not about race
jokes.punchline: Bluntly stating her personal dislike
jokes.explanation: Good Liz moment of brutal honesty trying to prove she's not racist

jokes.text: And maybe someday, our children or our children's children will hate each other like that. But it just doesn't work that way today.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if people can just dislike each other
jokes.punchline: Steven's backwards vision of future race relations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion of MLK's dream - hoping future generations can hate purely based on personality

jokes.text: No no no no no... Some women are gay.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if any woman who doesn't like him is racist
jokes.punchline: Steven's matter-of-fact acknowledgment of the only other possibility
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment showing Steven's limited worldview about rejection

jokes.text: Well, let me get on the black phone and call the NAACP so they could just send you your medal right now.
jokes.setup: Liz mentioning going to Source Awards
jokes.punchline: Steven's sarcastic response about getting anti-racism medal
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response with 'black phone' detail adding absurdity

jokes.text: Because I collect tote bags.
jokes.setup: Steven agreeing to go to Source Awards for the gift bag
jokes.punchline: Revealing his specific obsession with tote bags
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Steven's mundane interests

jokes.text: No--I think you like to dress black men up as Oprah as part of your effort to protect our dignity.
jokes.setup: Liz asking if Tracy thinks she's racist
jokes.punchline: Tracy's backwards logic about Oprah costume
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy logic - completely backwards interpretation of events

jokes.text: I'm gonna find a homeless man, dress him up like me, set him on fire, then I'm gonna start a brand-new life in Arizona under the new name 'Ron Mexico.'
jokes.setup: Tracy explaining his escape plan
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fake death scheme ending with specific fake identity
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tracy plan with perfect escalation from fake death to mundane new identity

jokes.text: Well, I think I speak for the both of us when I say because they're metal penises.
jokes.setup: Liz asking what it is with men and guns
jokes.punchline: Tracy's blunt Freudian analysis
jokes.explanation: Direct Freudian reference that fits Tracy's crude wisdom

jokes.text: What would Oprah do? Would she run away from her community, or would she face her problems head-on and try to make a difference at the Source Awards. What would I doooo!
jokes.setup: Liz invoking Oprah to motivate Tracy
jokes.punchline: Tracy channeling Oprah's dramatic speaking style
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Tracy's Oprah impression that drives the final act

jokes.text: What would I do, Tracy? What would I doooo!
jokes.setup: Tracy channeling Oprah
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top Oprah impression with extended 'do'
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Oprah impression, setting up Tracy's transformation

jokes.text: The monkey died of natural causes, so we're in the clear.
jokes.setup: Jack getting good news from the lab
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that they were testing on monkeys
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy throwaway about wine testing procedures

jokes.text: You're all my children of the corn.
jokes.setup: Tracy channeling Oprah at the Source Awards
jokes.punchline: Mixing Oprah's inspirational tone with horror movie reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment mixing Oprah inspiration with accidental horror

jokes.text: He's channeling Oprah. Why? Because he's articulate?
jokes.setup: Liz explaining Tracy's behavior
jokes.punchline: Steven's tone-deaf assumption about the reason
jokes.explanation: Great moment showing Steven's continued racial tone-deafness

jokes.text: You're all wonders. You're all my miracles. You're all my children of the corn.
jokes.setup: Tracy doing Oprah's positive affirmations
jokes.punchline: Ending with horror movie reference
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from Oprah positivity to horror movie reference

jokes.text: I actually tried some before. It tasted real good, but it make my tongue turn white.
jokes.setup: Someone praising Donaghy Estates wine
jokes.punchline: Gross side effect of drinking the terrible wine
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out callback reinforcing how awful the wine is

jokes.text: Got your nose! Jay, go get my nose back.
jokes.setup: Ridikolus doing the classic 'got your nose' trick
jokes.punchline: Taking it seriously and sending his bodyguard to retrieve it
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurd moment showing Ridikolus's childlike literal-mindedness

jokes.text: Because everyone is getting Vermont maple scooo-oones!
jokes.setup: Tracy channeling Oprah about looking under seats
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top delivery of mundane Vermont food item
jokes.explanation: Good Oprah parody with anti-climactic prize

jokes.text: Oh, will he be mad when you give them back?
jokes.setup: Thug saying P-Diddy wears these shoes
jokes.punchline: Kenneth assuming the shoes are borrowed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence misunderstanding gangsta posturing

jokes.text: Son, to have you, your moms must be so stupid she thinks Grape Nuts is an STD.
jokes.setup: Thug delivering an insult
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd example of stupidity
jokes.explanation: Solid street insult with clever wordplay on cereal name

jokes.text: Well, sir, your mother must not have raised you right, 'cause you're not saying very nice things.
jokes.setup: Kenneth responding to harsh insult
jokes.punchline: Polite, naive response treating gangsta beef like bad manners
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment turning street confrontation into etiquette lesson

jokes.text: You shot a Black! No no no no no-- It's cool; that's his last name.
jokes.setup: Liz accidentally shooting Steven
jokes.punchline: Jack's panicked reaction and quick clarification about the surname
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the name confusion in a moment of crisis

jokes.text: I got blood on my tote! He's got blood on his tote.
jokes.setup: Steven being shot
jokes.punchline: His primary concern being his tote bag collection
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Steven's priorities even when wounded

jokes.text: Wait till I tell Tupac about this.
jokes.setup: Ridikolus threatening consequences
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning telling the long-dead rapper
jokes.explanation: Great dark comedy moment treating deceased celebrity like current contact

jokes.text: Wait till I tell Tupac about this.
jokes.setup: Ridikolus angry about the chaos
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning telling dead rapper
jokes.explanation: Good throwaway joke suggesting Tupac is still alive in this universe
